system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

Cannot find file "points" in directory "polyMesh" in times "0" down to constant

    From function virtual Foam::IOobject Foam::fileOperation::findInstance(const Foam::IOobject&, Foam::scalar, const Foam::word&) const
    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Block hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) has inward-pointing faces
    4(8 12 15 11) 4(9 10 14 13) 4(8 9 13 12) 4(8 11 10 9) 

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_C_Cmodels/runs/squareBend/1/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 67.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 106.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Block 1 has "inward-pointing faces"

The error message is very clear:
```
Block hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15) ... has inward-pointing faces
    4(8 12 15 11) 4(9 10 14 13) 4(8 9 13 12) 4(8 11 10 9)
```

This means the vertex ordering in Block 1 produces faces that point inward instead of outward, violating OpenFOAM's requirement for proper right-hand-rule ordering.

## Root Cause

The problem is specifically with **Block 1** (the bend section):
```
hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15)
```

Let me trace through the vertex positions:
- Vertices 8–11 (bottom face): (25, 0, -25), (25, -50, -25), (25, -125, -25), (25, -125, 25)
- Vertices 12–15 (top face): (25, 0, 25), (25, -50, 25), (25, -125, 25), (25, -125, -25)

All vertices have x = 25! This means Block 1 is degenerate — it collapses into a flat plane because all four "bottom" vertices and all four "top" vertices lie on the same x-plane. A hexahedral block requires 8 distinct corner points forming a proper 3D parallelepiped. With all x-coordinates equal to 25, the block has zero thickness in the x-direction and cannot form a valid hex.

Similarly, **Block 0** also has issues — vertices 0–3 and 4–7 both span x=-50 to x=0, but the y-extent goes from -25 to 25 and z from -25 to 25. Looking at the vertex coordinates more carefully:

- Vertices 0(-50,25,-25), 1(-50,-25,-25), 2(0,-25,-25), 3(0,25,-25)
- Vertices 4(-50,25,25), 5(-50,-25,25), 6(0,-25,25), 7(0,25,25)

These define a proper rectangular prism from x=-50..0, y=-25..25, z=-25..25 — this one should be fine with correct ordering.

And **Block 2** has the same issue as Block 1 — vertices 16–19 and 20–23 all have x=25 except for vertices 18 and 19 at x=-500, creating a non-convex/collapsed shape.

## Recommended Solution

The fundamental problem is that the vertex layout doesn't produce valid 3D hex blocks. The squareBend tutorial uses a completely different vertex layout where blocks share faces properly and each hex has 8 geometrically valid corners.

Here’s what needs to change in `system/blockMeshDict`:

1. **Redesign the entire vertex layout** to match the squareBend tutorial structure. The tutorial uses a stepped/multi-block approach where:
   - Block 0: inlet duct (straight section before bend)
   - Block 1: the bend region (L-shaped transition)
   - Block 2: outlet duct (after the bend)
   
   The key difference is that in the squareBend tutorial, the bend involves a change in the **plane of flow** (from x-direction to y-direction), and the vertices are arranged so that adjacent blocks share full faces. The current attempt places all bend vertices at x=25, making Block 1 degenerate.

2. **For Block 1 (the bend)**, the bottom face should NOT have all vertices at x=25. Instead, the bend should curve in the xy-plane such that:
   - One face of the bend block connects to the end of Block 0 at x=0
   - Another face extends in the y-direction toward the outlet
   - The vertices should span multiple x-planes, not collapse to a single x-plane.

3. **Alternatively, simplify to a single-block approach**: Given the complexity of getting the multi-block topology right, consider designing a simpler single-block mesh where the entire domain is one hex block with appropriate edge grading. However, since the user specifies multiple regions and specific vertex locations, the multi-block approach is preferred.

The critical fix is ensuring that **every hex block has 8 vertices that form a proper convex 3D parallelepiped** — no collapsed or degenerate blocks allowed. Each block's local vertex ordering must follow the right-hand rule consistently across shared faces between blocks.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Replace the entire vertex list and block definitions with a corrected multi-block layout that forms valid 3D hexahedra. For Block 0 (inlet duct along x-axis): keep vertices 0-7 spanning x=-50 to 0, y=-25 to 25, z=-25 to 25. For Block 1 (bend section): redesign vertices 8-15 so they form a proper L-shaped bend connecting Block 0 to Block 2 - place vertices at x=0 to x=25 range with y changing from 0 to -50 and z from -25 to 25. For Block 2 (outlet duct along -y): redesign vertices 16-23 to span y=-50 to -125 and x=-500 to 25 with z=-25 to 25. Ensure all 8 vertices of each hex block are geometrically valid 3D corners and that adjacent blocks share matching faces. Keep the same boundary face definitions but update them to reference the new corrected vertex indices.'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
